items.title: More than 75 data center build-outs worth $130 billion have been successfully blocked in the first four months of 2026 — bipartisan opposition mounts nationwide over fears of soaring power and water costs
items.description: A research firm says the number of blocked data centers in the first quarter of 2026 already matches the number of projects stopped in 2025. The opposition also comes from both sides of the aisle, despite President Trump's push for more AI development inside the U.S.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center projects are being delayed nationwide as many opposition groups have successfully blocked their progress. According to Data Center Watch, a boutique research firm tracking data center development, at least 75 data center projects worth around $130 billion have been blocked or delayed across the nation in the first quarter of 2026 alone. By comparison, this is the same amount that was blocked throughout 2025.This metric reflects the growing opposition to data centers across the United States. In late 2025, an Ipsos survey showed that nearly half of Americans didn’t want a new data center project near their neighborhood. However, this number jumped to 70% a few months later, indicating increasing resistance to data centers. Many of the concerns raised by residents include massive electricity price hikes, egregious water consumption, and potential noise pollution, among others. However, there have also been reports that Chinese users have been amplifying these issues while posing as Americans.These concerns have resulted in numerous moratoriums passed in different jurisdictions. At least 69 local government units have enacted bans as of May 2026, with more joining the list since then. Seattle, the hometown of AI tech giants Microsoft and Amazon, is the biggest city to have passed a one-year pause on data center projects so far, affecting five proposed projects. It has even gotten to the point where Maine almost passed a statewide ban on large new data center projects until October 2027, with only the governor vetoing the bill because it affected one particular data center project.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Despite President Donald Trump’s push for AI development in the U.S., pushback against data centers has come from both sides of the aisle. While a few politicians have been pushing for data center development despite community opposition, many more have been listening to their constituents and passing regulations to halt or delay projects as they study how these projects could impact the community and mitigate any negative effects.The number of projects being blocked across the nation is concerning for both the federal government, which is competing against China to gain the edge in AI technology, and AI tech companies like Anthropic, which is struggling to gain access to more compute. But if these developments damage the environment and the lifestyle of the people living around them, then it seems that Americans would rather do without them. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 2026年前四个月，超过75个价值1300亿美元的数据中心扩建项目被成功阻止——因担忧电力和水成本飙升，全国两党反对声浪高涨
items.descriptionZh:
美国数据中心建设正遭遇前所未有的公众抵制浪潮。据专业研究机构Data Center Watch统计，仅2026年第一季度，全美就有至少75个数据中心项目（总价值约1300亿美元）被阻止或延期，这一数字已与2025年全年的受阻项目总量持平。该数据生动反映了美国民众对数据中心态度的急剧转变：2025年底Ipsos民调显示，近半数美国人反对在其社区附近新建数据中心；但仅数月后，这一比例已跃升至70%，表明抵制情绪正在快速蔓延。

居民反对的核心理由集中在三方面：电价大幅上涨（数据中心是典型高能耗设施）、惊人耗水量（冷却系统需大量水资源）以及潜在的噪音污染。值得注意的是，报道指出有迹象显示中国用户曾伪装成美国网民，在社交平台上放大这些争议议题。这些担忧已直接转化为政策行动——截至2026年5月，全美至少69个地方政府通过了数据中心禁令，更多辖区仍在跟进。其中最具标志性的是AI巨头微软和亚马逊的故乡西雅图，该市已通过为期一年的数据中心项目暂停令，导致5个拟建项目受阻。更为极端的是，缅因州差一点就通过了全州范围的大规模新数据中心禁令（有效期至2027年10月），最终仅因州长否决一项影响特定项目的条款才未生效。

尽管美国总统特朗普力推本土AI发展，但数据中心面临的阻力已跨越党派界限。部分政客力排众议推动项目上马，但更多官员选择倾听选民呼声，在评估项目对社区的影响并制定缓解方案之前，先通过法规暂停或延迟审批。这种全国性的项目受阻困境，既令旨在与中国争夺AI技术优势的联邦政府感到忧虑，也让像Anthropic这样亟需更多算力的AI公司陷入尴尬——算力扩张的物理基础正被民意动摇。

然而，当数据中心的建设被认为会破坏当地环境和居民生活方式时，美国人似乎更倾向于放弃这些设施。这场博弈的最终结果，将深刻影响美国AI基础设施的落地节奏与全球竞争力。

items.title: AMD challenges Nvidia's DGX Spark with $3,999 Ryzen AI Halo with Windows 11 support — Strix Halo desktop undercuts Nvidia by $700, packs 128GB of unified memory
items.description: Powered by the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor and 128GB of unified memory, AMD's developer kit arrives as a direct competitor to Nvidia's DGX Spark, which recently saw a price increase to $4,699.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform is finally here, with preorders for the powerful $3,999 mini PC now live in the U.S. through Micro Center. The company’s new developer platform is effectively an answer to Nvidia's DGX Spark, as both systems are designed as compact AI workstations capable of running large language models locally. First unveiled at CES 2026, the developer kit is available in two variants that feature identical hardware but ship with different operating systems- Linux or Windows 11 Pro.Notably, the DGX Spark was originally introduced at the same $3,999 price point last year. However, Nvidia recently increased the price to $4,699 due to the ongoing global supply constraints affecting LPDDR5X memory and NAND flash. Apart from pricing, the Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform offers native Windows support, whereas the DGX Spark is limited to Linux-based environments. This gives users greater flexibility, including the option to dual-boot and use both operating systems. (Image credit: AMD)The Ryzen AI Halo dev kit is powered by the flagship Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor, featuring 16 cores and 32 threads, with a base clock speed of 3 GHz and a max boost clock speed of 5.1 GHz. It also features 128GB of unified LPDDR5X-8000 memory, 16MB of L2 cache, and 64MB of L3 cache, onboard RDNA 3.5 Radeon 8060S graphics with 40 compute units, and a dedicated NPU capable of 50 TOPS.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The mini-PC measures 149 x 149 x 43.18 mm, has an aluminum chassis, and includes a 2TB M.2 SSD, while wireless connectivity options include Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4. As for I/O, it is strikingly similar to what Nvidia offers, including a 10 GbE LAN, four USB-C ports (one for power input), and an HDMI 2.1b port. Unfortunately, there is no NVLink, which, in Nvidia’s case, allows two DGK Spark machines to pair. To cool the Strix Halo chip inside AMD, it uses a unique cooling solution that includes a baseplate, direct-touch flat heatpipes, an aluminum channel heatsink, and two lateral airflow blower-style fans.If you are a developer or simply interested in experimenting with local LLMs, Micro Center is currently accepting preorders for the Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform, with local pickup available on July 10. Those looking for alternatives can also consider the Corsair AI Workstation 300, a slightly larger AI-focused system based on the same Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor. It is also currently listed at a lower starting price of $2,699 for the 1TB storage model and $3,399 for the 4TB model. items.author: Kunal Khullar
items.titleZh: AMD以3999美元Ryzen AI Halo挑战英伟达DGX Spark，支持Windows 11
items.descriptionZh:
AMD终于正式发布了其Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台，这款售价3999美元的迷你PC现在已在美国Micro Center开启预购。该平台是AMD对英伟达DGX Spark的直接回应——两者均被设计为紧凑型AI工作站，能够本地运行大型语言模型。该开发者套件首次在CES 2026上亮相，提供两种变体，硬件配置完全相同，但预装的操作系统不同：Linux版或Windows 11 Pro版。

值得注意的是，DGX Spark最初也以3999美元的价格在去年发布，但由于全球LPDDR5X内存和NAND闪存供应持续紧张，英伟达最近已将价格上调至4699美元。除了价格优势，Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台还原生支持Windows，而DGX Spark仅限于Linux环境。这为用户提供了更大的灵活性，包括支持双系统启动，可以同时使用两种操作系统。

核心硬件方面，该开发者套件搭载旗舰级Ryzen AI Max+ 395处理器，拥有16核32线程，基础频率3 GHz，最高加速频率5.1 GHz。它配备了128GB统一LPDDR5X-8000内存、16MB L2缓存和64MB L3缓存，以及集成RDNA 3.5架构的Radeon 8060S显卡（40个计算单元），同时还包含一个专用NPU，算力高达50 TOPS。

这款迷你PC的尺寸仅为149×149×43.18毫米，采用铝制机箱，内置一块2TB M.2 SSD，无线连接支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4。I/O接口方面与英伟达方案非常相似，包括一个10GbE LAN口、四个USB-C接口（其中一个用于供电）和一个HDMI 2.1b接口。不过，它缺少英伟达DGX Spark支持的NVLink（该技术允许两台DGX Spark机器配对使用）。为了给内部的Strix Halo芯片散热，AMD采用了独特的散热方案，包括底板、直接接触式扁平热管、铝制通道散热器和两个侧向气流鼓风机式风扇。

如果你是开发者或只是对本地运行大语言模型感兴趣，现在可以通过Micro Center预订Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台，并可在7月10日到店自提。对于寻求替代方案的用户，也可以考虑基于相同Ryzen AI Max+ 395处理器的Corsair AI Workstation 300，这款系统稍大一些，但起售价更低：1TB存储型号为2699美元，4TB型号为3399美元。

items.title: Nvidia raises RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU pricing to $13,250 — 55% increase over MSRP in a year's time
items.description: Nvidia now sells the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell graphics cards for $13,250, while partner offerings start at $11,359.99.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter No graphics card is safe from surging prices driven by the global memory shortage and the ongoing AI boom, whether it's one of the best graphics cards for gaming or workstations. Nvidia has quietly increased the price of the RTX Pro 6000, the flagship of its Blackwell workstation series, to a jaw-dropping $13,250, representing a 55% increase over its original launch price just one year ago.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCNvidia offers three different variants of the RTX Pro 6000 that cater to a wide range of consumers. The chipmaker designed the standard Workstation Edition and the more power-efficient Max-Q Workstation Edition for the professional segment. In contrast, the data center-oriented Server Edition targets large-scale enterprises.Nvidia launched the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell and RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q in March 2025. With a launch price of $8,565, it was always a premium product. Nonetheless, many consumers sought out the RTX Pro 6000 for its impressive performance in professional workloads.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: With Nvidia now asking $13,250 for the RTX Pro 6000, it has gone up by a whopping 55%. Nvidia lists the PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell at $11,359.99, which is 14% less than the MSRP. However, it’s important to note that these prices are the official listings for the RTX Pro 6000 on Nvidia’s marketplace; while a good reference, they do not always reflect the lowest available prices.Nvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell PricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyGraphics CardNvidiaNeweggB&H Photo VideoNvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell ServerN/A$14,999.00N/ANvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell$13,250.00$12,099.99$13,349.00Nvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q$13,250.00$14,999.00$13,999.00PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell$11,359.99$13,645.00$14,499.00PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-QN/AN/A$13,999.00For instance, Newegg currently sells the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell for $12,099.99, offering a 9% savings compared to Nvidia’s official marketplace price. When it comes to the other variants of the RTX Pro 6000, Nvidia’s marketplace offers lower prices than those found on Newegg or B&H Photo Video. Sometimes retailers are implementing significant markups, so it's more affordable to shop at Nvidia for certain models. While Nvidia doesn’t appear to offer the server variant directly to the general public, Newegg has it listed for a steep $14,999. You may like Framework's new RTX 5070 12GB graphics module costs a whopping $1,199 Corsair's Strix Halo AI Workstation 300 gets even more expensive amid the RAMpocalypse GPU price tracking 2026: Lowest price on every graphics card from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel today Some of these listings come from third-party sellers on Newegg, and others are marked as OEM units. OEM products are generally intended for system integrators or enterprise customers and are sold in bulk, so they typically don't come with the fancy retail packaging.Realistically, we shouldn’t expect graphics card pricing, whether for consumer gaming cards or professional workstation models, to improve any time soon. The only consistent trend is that prices will continue to climb until the memory shortage ends. Prices vary significantly across different retailers and even official marketplaces like Nvidia's. items.author: Zhiye Liu
items.titleZh: 英伟达将RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU定价提升至13250美元——一年内较建议零售价上涨55%
items.descriptionZh:
全球内存短缺与人工智能热潮持续推高显卡价格，即便是市面上最好的游戏或工作站级显卡也难以幸免。Nvidia 悄然将其 Blackwell 工作站系列旗舰产品 RTX Pro 6000 的价格提升至令人咋舌的 13,250 美元，较一年前的原始发布价上涨了 55%。

Nvidia 提供了三款不同的 RTX Pro 6000 变体，以满足广泛的用户需求。标准版工作站型号（Workstation Edition）和更节能的 Max-Q 工作站型号（Max-Q Workstation Edition）针对专业领域设计，而面向数据中心的服务器版本（Server Edition）则瞄准大型企业客户。

Nvidia 于 2025 年 3 月发布了 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 和 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q，当时首发价为 8,565 美元，这本身已属于高端产品。然而，许多用户因其在专业工作负载中的强劲性能而仍然选择购买。如今 Nvidia 将价格上调至 13,250 美元，涨幅高达 55%。Nvidia 官方市场列表上，PNY 品牌的 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 标价为 11,359.99 美元，比官方建议零售价低 14%。但需注意，这些价格是 Nvidia 官方市场的参考价，并不总是代表最低售价。

价格对比显示，不同零售商和渠道差异显著。例如，Newegg 目前将 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 标价为 12,099.99 美元，比 Nvidia 官方市场价便宜 9%。而对于其他变体，Nvidia 官方市场的价格反而低于 Newegg 或 B&H Photo Video 等零售商，有时零售商会大幅加价。Nvidia 似乎不直接向公众出售服务器版本，但 Newegg 上的第三方卖家将其标价为 14,999 美元。部分列表来自 Newegg 上的第三方卖家，其他标注为 OEM 产品——这些通常面向系统集成商或企业客户，以批量形式销售，不附带精美零售包装。

短期内，无论是消费级游戏显卡还是专业工作站显卡，价格都不太可能改善。唯一确定的趋势是：价格将继续攀升，直到内存短缺问题得到解决。不同零售商甚至官方市场（如 Nvidia 官网）之间的价格差异巨大，最佳策略是在购买前多方比价。

items.title: The future of Hollywood isn’t feeding prompts into vanilla gen AI models
items.description:
Concept art from Dear Upstairs Neighbors that used to train custom builds of Google’s Veo and Imagen models. | Image: Google DeepMind	

For all the noise that's been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven't really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the sort of entertainment people would pay to see. Most AI firms' video models are still only capable of churning out short bursts of visually inconsistent footage. And some of Hollywood's biggest AI partnerships have suddenly evaporated in ways that make it seem like studios might not be able to rely on the new technology coming out of Silicon Valley. For the most part, short-form video slop appears to be the only thing that major production houses ar …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: For all the noise that’s been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven’t really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the sort of entertainment people would pay to see. Most AI firms’ video models are still only capable of churning out short bursts of visually inconsistent footage. And some of Hollywood’s biggest AI partnerships have suddenly evaporated in ways that make it seem like studios might not be able to rely on the new technology coming out of Silicon Valley. For the most part, short-form video slop appears to be the only thing that major production houses are capable of cooking up with gen AI. But that could change if studios take notes from some of the experimental projects that debuted at this year’s Tribeca Film Festival.Though it feels unlikely that gen AI will ever be able to whip up a compelling movie whole cloth, Tribeca featured a number of films that demonstrated how human artists can leverage the technology in compelling ways.While none of the AI-powered movies that screened at Tribeca were as terrible as the video slop companies like OpenAI and xAI have polluted the internet with, some of the projects were prime examples of why generative content tends to feel so lifeless compared to human-crafted art. Roar — an animated short produced by Illuminai Studios — felt more like a disorienting montage of AI-generated clips rather than a cohesive piece of cinema. And Asteria Film Co.’s ChikaBOOM! lacked the visual and sonic polish that’s necessary for fast-paced fantasy about a magician in training to really pull you in.Roar and ChikaBOOM!’s overall roughness seemed to be a reflection of the inherent technological limitations baked into their respective AI-forward production workflows. But other films, like Google DeepMind’s Dear Upstairs Neighbors and OpenAI’s Mauvais Soleil, showcased how it’s possible for filmmakers to avoid those challenges when gen AI is deployed with a bit more ingenuity.Written and directed by Pixar veteran Connie Qin He in collaboration with researchers from Google DeepMind, Dear Upstairs Neighbors tells the story of an exhausted young woman who’s trying to go to bed. All Ada (Márcia Mayer, who also produced the short) wants is to get a couple hours of peaceful rest before she has to wake up and get back to work. But every time she begins dozing off, the cacophony of noise coming from her upstairs neighbors’ apartment jolts her awake and leaves her wondering what they could possibly be doing in the middle of the night.To give Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ world a distinct style, He enlisted Pixar production designer Yingzong Xin, who painted concept art in Photoshop and on paper using acrylics. Those illustrations’ expressionistic aesthetic was key to bringing Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ fantastical story to life, but it also presented a unique challenge to DeepMind’s researchers. With most AI video generation models, the illustrations’ painterly style would be difficult to turn into visually consistent footage. But DeepMind’s engineers developed custom versions of Veo and Imagen that were specifically designed to give Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ artists the ability to fine-tune their outputs.You can see how filmmakers have to work around some of gen AI’s more typical limitations.Because the customized models were trained on Xin’s concept art, they could consistently generate shots that adhered to He’s vision for the project. The text-to-video models were great at reproducing certain stylistic details, like the way sound is visualized when objects interact with one another. But to really build Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ scenes in a way that would tell a cohesive story, the short’s creative team had to do things a bit more traditionally. By creating rough animations with Autodesk Maya (the industry standard for 3D rigging and VFX), Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ production team could ensure that scenes would unfold exactly how they wanted them to. And by feeding those roughs into Veo, the artists could create scenes that were more visually polished and ready to be further enhanced with additional stylized assets generated with Veo and Imagen.More than any other film at Tribeca, Dear Upstairs Neighbors felt like a case study in how generative AI can be used as a bespoke tool that actually assists artists as they develop their ideas. The film’s entire workflow relied on human-made art and people making the kinds of nuanced creative decisions that text-to-video generators aren’t capable of on their own. It’s important to bear in mind that Dear Upstairs Neighbors would not be nearly as visually impressive if it had been produced with vanilla versions of Google’s various models. The models worked well for this particular short, but that’s to be expected for a project that’s also very much a commercial for Google’s technology.Image: Google DeepMindDear Upstairs Neighbors was a much more enjoyable watch than the films OpenAI brought to this year’s festival. Alice Gu’s semi-autobiographical drama Smoked used Sora to re-create the Palisades Fire, and Youssef Michraf’s Mauvais Soleil features a number of photorealistic scenes generated with OpenAI’s creative tools. Watching both films, you could see how their respective filmmakers had to work around some of gen AI’s more typical limitations. Wide shots in Smoked’s fiery scenes looked a bit cartoony, but the effect worked somewhat better in close-ups — which were filmed using a Volume-like setup — of a woman and her son trying to escape the blaze in their car. Most of Mauvais Soleil’s shots last for just a few seconds and the only speaking character is an unseen narrator, but the film’s story about how a man’s life is being warped by artificial intelligence makes those details feel like intentional artistic choices.OpenAI’s presence at Tribeca was somewhat surprising given the company’s recent decision to shut Sora down entirely. Sora’s sudden shuttering is what led to OpenAI’s feature-length film Critterz not being able to make its debut at this year’s Cannes Film Festival. It seems like OpenAI might be pivoting away from video-focused applications of its tech, but there are still other players in the gen AI space building tools that filmmakers can use to realize their projects.These workflows really only function well when they’re guided by human artists.With just $2,000 spent entirely on computing costs, writer / director Ash Koosha was able to singlehandedly produce Dreams of Violets, a docudrama focused on the nationwide protests that have rocked Iran throughout the past year. Using Kling AI, Claude, Gemini, and Nano Banana, Koosha tells a fictionalized story about a group of people who find themselves trapped in an alley as police stalk the streets brutalizing civilians. The project took Koosha just a few weeks to finish by himself, and while it’s supported by a powerful narrative, it doesn’t break any ground visually.Watching all of these films, I got the distinct sense that there is no future where studios are cranking out commercially viable projects by feeding prompts to gen AI models. That kind of content probably isn’t going to go away, but it’s not the kind of stuff Hollywood’s heavyweights would want to put their names on. What seems much more likely is bigger AI firms like Google partnering with studios to build bespoke models that are tailored to very specific workflows. And those workflows really only function well when they’re guided by human artists with very clear creative visions.
items.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
items.titleZh: 好莱坞的未来不是向普通生成式AI模型输入提示词
items.descriptionZh:
生成式AI一直被宣传为将彻底改变电影行业，但迄今为止，几乎没有用这项技术制作出让人愿意付费观看的娱乐作品。大多数AI公司的视频模型仍只能生成时长极短、视觉风格不连贯的片段。好莱坞一些最大的AI合作项目突然消失，也让人怀疑制片厂是否还能依赖硅谷的新技术。总体来看，大型制片公司用生成式AI能搞出来的似乎只有短视频“垃圾”。不过，如果制片厂能从今年翠贝卡电影节上展出的实验性项目中吸取经验，这一局面或许会改变。

虽然生成式AI似乎不太可能凭空创作出引人入胜的完整电影，但翠贝卡电影节上的多部影片展示了人类艺术家如何以巧妙的方式利用这项技术。这些AI驱动影片没有像OpenAI和xAI等公司污染互联网的视频“垃圾”那样糟糕，但有些项目也恰恰揭示了为什么生成式内容往往比人类创作的艺术显得更缺乏生命力。例如，Illuminai Studios制作的动画短片《Roar》更像一段令人困惑的AI生成片段蒙太奇，而非连贯的电影作品；Asteria Film Co.的《ChikaBOOM!》则缺乏视觉和声音上的打磨，难以吸引观众沉浸在一个关于见习魔法师的快节奏奇幻故事中。

《Roar》和《ChikaBOOM!》的粗糙感似乎反映了各自AI优先制作流程中固有的技术局限。而其他影片，如Google DeepMind的《亲爱的楼上邻居》（Dear Upstairs Neighbors）和OpenAI的《坏太阳》（Mauvais Soleil），则展示了当生成式AI被更巧妙地部署时，电影制作人能够规避这些挑战。

《亲爱的楼上邻居》由皮克斯资深人士秦何（Connie Qin He）编剧并执导，与Google DeepMind的研究人员合作完成。影片讲述了一位疲惫的年轻女性试图入睡的故事。主角艾达（由制片人Márcia Mayer配音）只想在起床继续工作前安静休息几个小时，但楼上邻居公寓传来的嘈杂噪音每次都把她惊醒，让她怀疑他们半夜到底在干什么。为了给影片的世界赋予独特风格，秦何邀请了皮克斯制作设计师辛英宗（Yingzong Xin）用Photoshop和丙烯画纸创作概念艺术。这些插画的表現主义美学是赋予影片奇幻故事生命力的关键，但也给DeepMind的研究人员带来了独特挑战。大多数AI视频生成模型难以将这种绘画风格转化为视觉一致的镜头。然而，DeepMind的工程师开发了定制版本的Veo和Imagen，专门让影片的艺术家能够精细调整输出结果。

由于定制模型是在辛的概念艺术上训练的，它们能够一致地生成符合秦何项目愿景的镜头。这些文本到视频模型擅长再现某些风格细节，比如物体相互作用时声音的可视化方式。但为了真正构建出能讲述连贯故事的场景，短片创作团队不得不采用更传统的方法。通过使用Autodesk Maya（3D蒙皮和视觉特效的行业标准）制作粗略动画，团队确保场景按预期展开。然后将这些粗略动画输入Veo，艺术家就能创建视觉上更精致的场景，并进一步用Veo和Imagen生成的额外风格化资产进行增强。

在翠贝卡电影节的众多影片中，《亲爱的楼上邻居》最像是一个案例研究，展示了生成式AI如何作为定制工具真正协助艺术家发展创意。整部影片的工作流程都依赖于人类创作的艺术以及人类做出的细微创意决策——这是文本到视频生成器无法独立完成的。值得注意的是，如果使用谷歌模型的原生版本，《亲爱的楼上邻居》在视觉上绝不会如此令人印象深刻。这些模型对这个特定短片很有效，但这也在意料之中，毕竟该项目本身也是谷歌技术的宣传片。

相比之下，OpenAI带来的影片观赏体验稍逊。艾丽斯·古（Alice Gu）的半自传体剧情片《Smoked》使用Sora再现了帕利塞兹大火；尤瑟夫·米沙拉夫（Youssef Michraf）的《坏太阳》则包含大量用OpenAI创意工具生成的逼真场景。观看这两部影片时，可以看到电影制作人如何规避生成式AI的一些典型局限。《Smoked》中火灾场景的广角镜头看起来有点卡通化，但在一个女人和儿子试图驾车逃离火灾的特写镜头中——这些镜头是用类似Volume的装置拍摄的——效果反而稍好。《坏太阳》的大多数镜头只持续几秒，唯一的对白角色是一位看不见的旁白，但影片关于一个男人的生活被人工智能扭曲的故事，让这些细节显得像是刻意的艺术选择。

OpenAI出现在翠贝卡电影节有些令人惊讶，因为该公司最近决定彻底关闭Sora。Sora的突然关闭正是导致OpenAI的长片《Critterz》无法在今年戛纳电影节首映的原因。OpenAI似乎正在远离其技术的视频应用，但生成式AI领域还有其他玩家在开发电影制作人可用于实现项目的工具。

作家兼导演阿什·库沙（Ash Koosha）仅用了2000美元计算成本，就独立制作了纪录片式剧情片《紫罗兰之梦》（Dreams of Violets），聚焦过去一年席卷伊朗的全国性抗议。他使用Kling AI、Claude、Gemini和Nano Banana，讲述了一个虚构故事：一群人发现自己被困在一条小巷中，而警察在街上横行，残暴对待平民。该项目仅用了几周时间就由他独自完成，虽然叙事有力，但在视觉上并未突破。

观看所有这些影片后，我得到一个清晰的感觉：制片厂通过向生成式AI模型输入提示语来产出商业上可行的项目的未来并不存在。那种内容可能不会消失，但好莱坞巨头不会愿意在这种东西上署名。更可能的是，像谷歌这样的大型AI公司与制片厂合作，构建针对特定工作流程的定制模型。而这些工作流程只有在非常清晰创意愿景的人类艺术家引导下，才能真正良好运作。

items.title: Dev releases ‘unblockable’ ASCII video stream software, stoking fears of unstoppable ads — delivers 360p video at 30 FPS and acts as a ‘bridge for AI’
items.description: A new and unique video streaming solution is pitched as a 'high-performance, real-time ASCII video rendering engine' that can be used to broadcast 'an unblockable video stream.'
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new and unique ASCII video streaming solution has been released under the MIT license. ASCILINE Engine by YusufB5 is pitched as a “high-performance, real-time ASCII video rendering engine” that can be used to broadcast “an unblockable video stream.” Examples of its capabilities are provided in the linked GitHub repository and social media posts by the dev, like the one embedded below. It's also stoked a bit of controversy due to fears of it potentially enabling unblockable ads.I built an unblockable video stream. It renders 360p at 30 FPS using pure text instead of tags. from r/SideProjectFrom our perusal of the examples, ASCILINE does indeed look like it is capable of better fidelity than prior video to ASCII streamers, some of which have a surprisingly long history dating back to the 90s. The software does a pretty decent job of making color text-based videos from a source. The dev notes that this technique uses Mode 3, using a palette of 32K colors, and can output at 30 FPS. Though classic mono ASCII is also a render option.Most impressive is the so-called real-time pixel streaming. The GitHub explains that this technique uses Mode 5 and “replaces characters with colored blocks, approaching 360p video quality.” Indeed, in the small embedded video on the page, it looks indistinguishable from the source MP4. We think the innate blockiness would become apparent quickly if it were rendered in a larger window, though.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: YusufB5 on GitHub)In a mission statement of sorts, YusufB5 says that ASCILINE’s “core objective is to transform the web into a highly dynamic and interactive typographic canvas. By mapping pixels to text-based representations, we unlock new possibilities for web media delivery.” On social media, the dev simplifies this highfalutin vision by heralding ASCILINE Engine as a tool that can build “an unblockable video stream. It renders 360p at 30 FPS using pure text.”The ‘unblockable’ claim gets plenty of pushback across various social media channels. For example, an adblocker put in element zapper mode can quickly remove the HTML5 Canvas that the ASCII video renders in. That’s just one option. You may like Developer creates a basic first person shooter game using Gaussian splats, and you can play it for free in your browser Nvidia's own DLSS 5 announcement video gets taken down by YouTube in Italy due to a copyright strike UK spy agency releases malware-blocking gadget for HDMI and DisplayPort cables However, most commenters are fearful of those wanting to serve more ads to the public using this ‘unblockable’ tech. YusufB5 points to their “strict anti-ad clause to the MIT License to ensure this isn't abused to force unskippable ads on people.” That might work with registered companies, but malicious users won’t care about such a rule.Beyond the unblockability and ad misuse controversies, ASCILINE has some more interesting frills and features to set it apart from the old guard of ASCII video. The ability to apply real-time CSS filters to the video stream sounds like it may be appealing in some instances. Also, the ASCII video generated is thought to “act as a perfect bridge for AI.” Thus, you can use lightweight LLMs to process semantic video summaries. The engine’s “ultra-low bandwidth & IoT compatibility” may also be attractive to some, with its ability to stream at only a few kilobytes per frame. Sending only the characters that change (delta frames) and applying GZIP compression both help in this regard.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: 开发者发布“不可屏蔽”的ASCII视频流软件，引发对无法阻挡的广告的担忧——以30 FPS传输360p视频，并充当“人工智能的桥梁”
items.descriptionZh:
基于MIT许可协议发布的新型ASCII视频流解决方案——ASCILINE Engine，由开发者YusufB5推出，被宣传为一款“高性能、实时ASCII视频渲染引擎”，能够传输“不可阻挡的视频流”。该项目已在GitHub上开源，并引发了关于其可能被滥用于推送不可阻挡广告的争议。

### 背景与上下文
传统的ASCII视频流技术可追溯至20世纪90年代，但受限于分辨率和色彩表现。ASCILINE Engine试图突破这些限制，通过纯文本字符映射实现接近标准视频的质量。开发者声称其核心目标是“将网页转变为高度动态、交互式的排版画布”，从而为网页媒体传输解锁新可能。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
引擎支持两种渲染模式：
- **Mode 3（经典ASCII模式）**：使用32K色彩调色板（15位色），以30帧/秒输出彩色字符视频。该模式保留了传统ASCII艺术风格，但显著提升了色彩精度。
- **Mode 5（实时像素流模式）**：将字符替换为彩色方块，以“缺失分辨率换取流畅度”，实现接近360p的视频质量。在该模式下，字符网格中的每个单元格被填充为纯色块，从而模拟像素视觉。演示视频显示，在小窗口渲染中，其效果与原始MP4几乎无法区分，但放大后锯齿感可能显露。

创新点包括：
- **增量帧传输**：仅发送发生变化的字符（delta frames），结合GZIP压缩，将每帧数据传输量降至数KB级别，实现超低带宽消耗。
- **物联网兼容性**：由于数据量极小，可在低功耗设备或窄带网络上流畅运行。
- **实时CSS滤镜**：支持对视频流应用CSS过滤器，提供动态视觉效果调整能力。
- **AI友好接口**：生成的结构化文本视频可作为轻量级大语言模型（LLM）处理语义摘要的“完美桥梁”，允许基于文本形式的视频内容分析。

### 性能参数与对比数据
- 分辨率：Mode 5模式下可达360p（约480×360字符网格，每个字符视为像素块）。
- 帧率：稳定30 FPS，与标准视频流一致。
- 带宽：每帧仅需数KB（压缩后），远低于传统视频流（通常需数百KB至数MB）。
- 色彩：Mode 3支持32000色；Mode 5使用有限色块（像素块颜色取自源视频平均色）。

与历史ASCII视频方案相比，ASCILINE在色彩保真度和实时性上有显著提升，尤其Mode 5的像素块渲染技术实现了“视觉上难以区分”的效果，这在此前是难以做到的。

### 技术影响与应用场景
1. **“不可阻挡”的争议**：开发者宣称该引擎可构建“不可阻挡的视频流”，因为内容以纯文本形式传输，传统广告拦截器难以识别。然而，技术社区指出，广告拦截器可通过“元素消隐”模式（如删除HTML5 Canvas元素）轻松阻止渲染。尽管如此，核心担忧在于该技术可能被滥用于强制推送无法跳过的广告。开发者已在MIT许可中加入了“严格反广告条款”，但该条款对恶意用户缺乏约束力。
2. **潜在合法应用**：
   - **低带宽直播**：适用于网络条件受限的场景（如农村、车载、IoT设备），仅需基础文本通道即可传输视频。
   - **AI视频摘要**：将视频转换为文本流后，可由轻量级LLM实时生成语义摘要，实现无障碍访问或快速浏览。
   - **创意艺术**：生成具有ASCII美学的动态视频，可用于复古界面、数字艺术或特殊视觉效果。
   - **教育工具**：无需复杂编解码器即可展示视频内容，适合浏览器兼容性要求高的环境。

### 社区反馈与未来展望
尽管技术演示令人印象深刻，但质疑声集中于实际可用性。例如，360p分辨率在放大后明显不足，且实时渲染依赖HTML5 Canvas，导致其“不可阻挡”特性易被绕开。然而，ASCILINE Engine展示了将视频降维至文本层的新思路，可能启发未来更多文本化媒体处理方案。目前，项目已获得Reddit、Tom's Hardware等科技媒体关注，成为ASCII视频演进史上的一个标志性节点。

items.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Leads on First Agentic AI Infrastructure Benchmark
items.description: AgentPerf from Artificial Analysis, the industry’s first agentic AI benchmark, gives developers, enterprises and infrastructure providers a clear way to compare systems for agentic AI. In the first round of published results, the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72 platform delivers leading performance across the agentic AI workloads tested, running 20x more agents per megawatt than NVIDIA […]
items.content: AgentPerf from Artificial Analysis, the industry’s first agentic AI benchmark, gives developers, enterprises and infrastructure providers a clear way to compare systems for agentic AI. In the first round of published results, the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72 platform delivers leading performance across the agentic AI workloads tested, running 20x more agents per megawatt than NVIDIA Hopper. Agentic AI is a fundamentally different workload than conversational AI. A single chat completion is a sprint: one large language model (LLM) call, one response. An agent functions more like a relay: It breaks a goal into many steps and keeps going until the task is done. Agents chain together multiple LLM calls and tool calls to gather context, observe, reason and act. That results in dozens to hundreds of LLM calls chained together, each passing growing context to the next, with tool calls like code compile and execution, database search and web browsing at every handoff. The complexity isn’t additive; it’s multiplicative. The distinction matters enormously for performance measurement. Existing AI inference benchmarks measure one LLM call: how fast an LLM responds to a single request and how many simultaneous requests a system can handle. They weren’t designed for agentic workloads, where chained LLM calls, tool call delays and growing context stress accelerated computing systems in fundamentally different ways than a single LLM call ever could. For companies building and deploying agents at scale, it’s important to understand how responsive agents are, how many can be deployed simultaneously and how much useful work AI infrastructure can deliver for every dollar and watt invested. NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 Runs 20x More Agents per Megawatt In this first round, AgentPerf measures agentic performance with DeepSeek V4 Pro, a large mixture-of-experts (MoE) model that represents the class of frontier models powering today’s most capable agents. On this workload, NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 delivers the highest performance in the benchmark, running up to 20x more agents per megawatt than the NVIDIA HGX H200 system. NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 supports far more concurrent agents per megawatt than NVIDIA H200 at both service-level objectives of 20 and 60 tokens per second per agent. The performance advantage comes from extreme codesign across the full stack. GB300 NVL72 connects 72 GPUs into a single rack-scale system, enabling large MoE models like DeepSeek V4 Pro to distribute model execution efficiently at scale. CUDA kernels accelerate this further by overlapping communication and compute, so the cost of coordinating across experts is absorbed rather than added to latency. NVIDIA TensorRT LLM sustains efficiency as concurrent agent sessions scale. For example, it separates the processing of inputs from the generation of outputs so each can be optimized independently. These results are grounded in a benchmark methodology built from the ground up to reflect how agentic AI actually works in production. Artificial Analysis AgentPerf: Built on Real-World Agentic Workloads AgentPerf is built based on real coding agent trajectories: an agent receives a task, reads files, writes and edits code, executes commands and iterates based on the results — all drawn from real public code repositories across 12+ programming languages. The long sequence lengths, tool call patterns and delays are all representative of real-world coding workflows. AgentPerf then measures how many of these agentic tasks a platform can support simultaneously while meeting defined performance thresholds for responsiveness and output token rate. Tool calls are not executed but simulated using representative CPU processing time, so differences in results reflect accelerated computing performance only. The results translate directly into infrastructure decisions: how many concurrent agentic tasks can be run per accelerator and per megawatt of power. For enterprises deploying AI agents at scale, those numbers determine how much productive work a given infrastructure investment can actually deliver. NVIDIA Ecosystem Partners Harness Blackwell’s Leading Performance Leading inference providers including Baseten, DeepInfra and Together AI are already serving agentic workloads on frontier models such as DeepSeek V4 Pro on NVIDIA Blackwell and powering production agentic applications today. Together AI powers real-time inference for Cursor, an AI-powered agentic coding platform, on NVIDIA Blackwell. Cursor’s agents debug issues, generate features and execute refactors while developers continue working. DeepInfra powers Pam.ai, an AI workforce platform for car dealerships, which deploys agents to book service appointments, handle calls and run outbound sales campaigns, entirely on NVIDIA Blackwell. As NVIDIA and the open source ecosystem continue to optimize inference software, performance and efficiency on agentic workloads will only improve. The NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture is now in full production, bringing the next generation of infrastructure capacity to meet the growing demands of agentic AI at scale. Dive deeper into AgentPerf’s methodology and NVIDIA’s full-stack optimizations for agentic AI in this technical blog.
items.author: Shruti Koparkar
items.titleZh: NVIDIA Blackwell 在首个代理型 AI 基础设施基准测试中领先
items.descriptionZh:
业界首个智能体AI（Agentic AI）基准测试AgentPerf由Artificial Analysis推出，为开发者、企业和基础设施提供商提供了一种清晰对比智能体AI系统性能的方式。在首轮公布的结果中，NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72平台在测试的智能体AI工作负载上取得了领先性能，每兆瓦功耗可运行20倍于NVIDIA Hopper的智能体数量。

智能体AI与对话式AI是截然不同的工作负载。单次对话补全如同短跑：一次大语言模型（LLM）调用，一次响应。而智能体更像是接力赛：它将一个目标分解为多个步骤，不断执行直到任务完成。智能体会串联多次LLM调用和工具调用，以收集上下文、观察、推理并采取行动。这导致数十到数百次LLM调用链条式连接，每次调用都在传递不断增长的上下文，并在每次交接时执行代码编译、执行、数据库搜索、网页浏览等工具调用。其复杂度不是相加的，而是相乘的。

这种差异对性能测量至关重要。现有的AI推理基准只测量单次LLM调用：衡量LLM对单一请求的响应速度以及系统能同时处理多少并发请求。它们并非为智能体工作负载设计，因为链式LLM调用、工具调用延迟和不断增长的上下文对加速计算系统的压力方式与单次LLM调用有本质不同。对于大规模构建和部署智能体的企业来说，了解智能体的响应速度、能同时部署多少个、以及每投入一美元和每瓦特功耗能产出的有用工作量，至关重要。

NVIDIA GB300 NVL72每兆瓦运行20倍智能体数
在本轮测试中，AgentPerf使用DeepSeek V4 Pro（一款大型混合专家模型，代表当前驱动最强大智能体的前沿模型类别）来衡量智能体性能。在该工作负载下，NVIDIA GB300 NVL72在基准测试中实现了最高性能，每兆瓦功耗运行的智能体数量达到NVIDIA HGX H200系统的20倍。在每智能体每秒20个token和60个token两个服务水平目标下，GB300 NVL72每兆瓦支持的并发智能体数均远超H200。

这一性能优势源于全栈极致协同设计。GB300 NVL72将72块GPU连接成一个机架级系统，使DeepSeek V4 Pro这类大型MoE模型能够高效地大规模分布式执行。CUDA内核进一步通过重叠通信和计算来加速，使得跨专家协调的成本被吸收而非叠加到延迟中。NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM在并发智能体会话扩展时保持效率，例如它将输入处理与输出生成分离，使两者可独立优化。

这些结果根植于一个从零开始构建、反映智能体AI在生产中实际运作方式的基准方法。

AgentPerf：基于真实世界智能体工作负载构建
AgentPerf基于真实编码智能体的轨迹构建：智能体接收任务、读取文件、编写和编辑代码、执行命令并根据结果迭代——所有这些都来自12种以上编程语言的真实公共代码仓库。长序列长度、工具调用模式和延迟均代表真实编码工作流。然后AgentPerf衡量一个平台能在满足响应性和输出token率定义阈值的同时，并行支持多少此类智能体任务。工具调用不实际执行，而是用代表性的CPU处理时间模拟，因此结果差异仅反映加速计算性能。

结果直接转化为基础设施决策：每个加速器和每兆瓦功耗可运行多少并发智能体任务。对于大规模部署AI智能体的企业，这些数字决定了给定基础设施投资能实际产生多少有效工作量。

NVIDIA生态伙伴利用Blackwell领先性能
领先的推理提供商如Baseten、DeepInfra和Together AI已经在NVIDIA Blackwell上使用DeepSeek V4 Pro等前沿模型为智能体工作负载提供服务，并驱动生产级智能体应用。Together AI为AI编码平台Cursor在Blackwell上提供实时推理，Cursor的智能体能在开发者继续工作的同时调试问题、生成功能和执行重构。DeepInfra为汽车经销商的AI劳动力平台Pam.ai提供支持，该平台在Blackwell上部署智能体来预约服务、处理电话和进行外呼营销。

随着NVIDIA和开源生态系统继续优化推理软件，智能体工作负载的性能和效率将持续提升。NVIDIA Vera Rubin架构现已全面投产，为满足大规模智能体AI日益增长的需求带来了下一代基础设施能力。更多关于AgentPerf方法及NVIDIA全栈优化的细节可参阅技术博客。

history.title: More than 75 data center build-outs worth $130 billion have been successfully blocked in the first four months of 2026 — bipartisan opposition mounts nationwide over fears of soaring power and water costs
history.description: A research firm says the number of blocked data centers in the first quarter of 2026 already matches the number of projects stopped in 2025. The opposition also comes from both sides of the aisle, despite President Trump's push for more AI development inside the U.S.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center projects are being delayed nationwide as many opposition groups have successfully blocked their progress. According to Data Center Watch, a boutique research firm tracking data center development, at least 75 data center projects worth around $130 billion have been blocked or delayed across the nation in the first quarter of 2026 alone. By comparison, this is the same amount that was blocked throughout 2025.This metric reflects the growing opposition to data centers across the United States. In late 2025, an Ipsos survey showed that nearly half of Americans didn’t want a new data center project near their neighborhood. However, this number jumped to 70% a few months later, indicating increasing resistance to data centers. Many of the concerns raised by residents include massive electricity price hikes, egregious water consumption, and potential noise pollution, among others. However, there have also been reports that Chinese users have been amplifying these issues while posing as Americans.These concerns have resulted in numerous moratoriums passed in different jurisdictions. At least 69 local government units have enacted bans as of May 2026, with more joining the list since then. Seattle, the hometown of AI tech giants Microsoft and Amazon, is the biggest city to have passed a one-year pause on data center projects so far, affecting five proposed projects. It has even gotten to the point where Maine almost passed a statewide ban on large new data center projects until October 2027, with only the governor vetoing the bill because it affected one particular data center project.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Despite President Donald Trump’s push for AI development in the U.S., pushback against data centers has come from both sides of the aisle. While a few politicians have been pushing for data center development despite community opposition, many more have been listening to their constituents and passing regulations to halt or delay projects as they study how these projects could impact the community and mitigate any negative effects.The number of projects being blocked across the nation is concerning for both the federal government, which is competing against China to gain the edge in AI technology, and AI tech companies like Anthropic, which is struggling to gain access to more compute. But if these developments damage the environment and the lifestyle of the people living around them, then it seems that Americans would rather do without them. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 2026年前四个月，超过75个价值1300亿美元的数据中心扩建项目被成功阻止——因担忧电力和水成本飙升，全国两党反对声浪高涨
history.descriptionZh:
美国数据中心建设正遭遇前所未有的公众抵制浪潮。据专业研究机构Data Center Watch统计，仅2026年第一季度，全美就有至少75个数据中心项目（总价值约1300亿美元）被阻止或延期，这一数字已与2025年全年的受阻项目总量持平。该数据生动反映了美国民众对数据中心态度的急剧转变：2025年底Ipsos民调显示，近半数美国人反对在其社区附近新建数据中心；但仅数月后，这一比例已跃升至70%，表明抵制情绪正在快速蔓延。

居民反对的核心理由集中在三方面：电价大幅上涨（数据中心是典型高能耗设施）、惊人耗水量（冷却系统需大量水资源）以及潜在的噪音污染。值得注意的是，报道指出有迹象显示中国用户曾伪装成美国网民，在社交平台上放大这些争议议题。这些担忧已直接转化为政策行动——截至2026年5月，全美至少69个地方政府通过了数据中心禁令，更多辖区仍在跟进。其中最具标志性的是AI巨头微软和亚马逊的故乡西雅图，该市已通过为期一年的数据中心项目暂停令，导致5个拟建项目受阻。更为极端的是，缅因州差一点就通过了全州范围的大规模新数据中心禁令（有效期至2027年10月），最终仅因州长否决一项影响特定项目的条款才未生效。

尽管美国总统特朗普力推本土AI发展，但数据中心面临的阻力已跨越党派界限。部分政客力排众议推动项目上马，但更多官员选择倾听选民呼声，在评估项目对社区的影响并制定缓解方案之前，先通过法规暂停或延迟审批。这种全国性的项目受阻困境，既令旨在与中国争夺AI技术优势的联邦政府感到忧虑，也让像Anthropic这样亟需更多算力的AI公司陷入尴尬——算力扩张的物理基础正被民意动摇。

然而，当数据中心的建设被认为会破坏当地环境和居民生活方式时，美国人似乎更倾向于放弃这些设施。这场博弈的最终结果，将深刻影响美国AI基础设施的落地节奏与全球竞争力。

history.title: AMD challenges Nvidia's DGX Spark with $3,999 Ryzen AI Halo with Windows 11 support — Strix Halo desktop undercuts Nvidia by $700, packs 128GB of unified memory
history.description: Powered by the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor and 128GB of unified memory, AMD's developer kit arrives as a direct competitor to Nvidia's DGX Spark, which recently saw a price increase to $4,699.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform is finally here, with preorders for the powerful $3,999 mini PC now live in the U.S. through Micro Center. The company’s new developer platform is effectively an answer to Nvidia's DGX Spark, as both systems are designed as compact AI workstations capable of running large language models locally. First unveiled at CES 2026, the developer kit is available in two variants that feature identical hardware but ship with different operating systems- Linux or Windows 11 Pro.Notably, the DGX Spark was originally introduced at the same $3,999 price point last year. However, Nvidia recently increased the price to $4,699 due to the ongoing global supply constraints affecting LPDDR5X memory and NAND flash. Apart from pricing, the Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform offers native Windows support, whereas the DGX Spark is limited to Linux-based environments. This gives users greater flexibility, including the option to dual-boot and use both operating systems. (Image credit: AMD)The Ryzen AI Halo dev kit is powered by the flagship Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor, featuring 16 cores and 32 threads, with a base clock speed of 3 GHz and a max boost clock speed of 5.1 GHz. It also features 128GB of unified LPDDR5X-8000 memory, 16MB of L2 cache, and 64MB of L3 cache, onboard RDNA 3.5 Radeon 8060S graphics with 40 compute units, and a dedicated NPU capable of 50 TOPS.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The mini-PC measures 149 x 149 x 43.18 mm, has an aluminum chassis, and includes a 2TB M.2 SSD, while wireless connectivity options include Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4. As for I/O, it is strikingly similar to what Nvidia offers, including a 10 GbE LAN, four USB-C ports (one for power input), and an HDMI 2.1b port. Unfortunately, there is no NVLink, which, in Nvidia’s case, allows two DGK Spark machines to pair. To cool the Strix Halo chip inside AMD, it uses a unique cooling solution that includes a baseplate, direct-touch flat heatpipes, an aluminum channel heatsink, and two lateral airflow blower-style fans.If you are a developer or simply interested in experimenting with local LLMs, Micro Center is currently accepting preorders for the Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform, with local pickup available on July 10. Those looking for alternatives can also consider the Corsair AI Workstation 300, a slightly larger AI-focused system based on the same Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor. It is also currently listed at a lower starting price of $2,699 for the 1TB storage model and $3,399 for the 4TB model. history.author: Kunal Khullar
history.titleZh: AMD以3999美元Ryzen AI Halo挑战英伟达DGX Spark，支持Windows 11
history.descriptionZh:
AMD终于正式发布了其Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台，这款售价3999美元的迷你PC现在已在美国Micro Center开启预购。该平台是AMD对英伟达DGX Spark的直接回应——两者均被设计为紧凑型AI工作站，能够本地运行大型语言模型。该开发者套件首次在CES 2026上亮相，提供两种变体，硬件配置完全相同，但预装的操作系统不同：Linux版或Windows 11 Pro版。

值得注意的是，DGX Spark最初也以3999美元的价格在去年发布，但由于全球LPDDR5X内存和NAND闪存供应持续紧张，英伟达最近已将价格上调至4699美元。除了价格优势，Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台还原生支持Windows，而DGX Spark仅限于Linux环境。这为用户提供了更大的灵活性，包括支持双系统启动，可以同时使用两种操作系统。

核心硬件方面，该开发者套件搭载旗舰级Ryzen AI Max+ 395处理器，拥有16核32线程，基础频率3 GHz，最高加速频率5.1 GHz。它配备了128GB统一LPDDR5X-8000内存、16MB L2缓存和64MB L3缓存，以及集成RDNA 3.5架构的Radeon 8060S显卡（40个计算单元），同时还包含一个专用NPU，算力高达50 TOPS。

这款迷你PC的尺寸仅为149×149×43.18毫米，采用铝制机箱，内置一块2TB M.2 SSD，无线连接支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4。I/O接口方面与英伟达方案非常相似，包括一个10GbE LAN口、四个USB-C接口（其中一个用于供电）和一个HDMI 2.1b接口。不过，它缺少英伟达DGX Spark支持的NVLink（该技术允许两台DGX Spark机器配对使用）。为了给内部的Strix Halo芯片散热，AMD采用了独特的散热方案，包括底板、直接接触式扁平热管、铝制通道散热器和两个侧向气流鼓风机式风扇。

如果你是开发者或只是对本地运行大语言模型感兴趣，现在可以通过Micro Center预订Ryzen AI Halo开发者平台，并可在7月10日到店自提。对于寻求替代方案的用户，也可以考虑基于相同Ryzen AI Max+ 395处理器的Corsair AI Workstation 300，这款系统稍大一些，但起售价更低：1TB存储型号为2699美元，4TB型号为3399美元。

history.title: Nvidia raises RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU pricing to $13,250 — 55% increase over MSRP in a year's time
history.description: Nvidia now sells the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell graphics cards for $13,250, while partner offerings start at $11,359.99.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter No graphics card is safe from surging prices driven by the global memory shortage and the ongoing AI boom, whether it's one of the best graphics cards for gaming or workstations. Nvidia has quietly increased the price of the RTX Pro 6000, the flagship of its Blackwell workstation series, to a jaw-dropping $13,250, representing a 55% increase over its original launch price just one year ago.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCNvidia offers three different variants of the RTX Pro 6000 that cater to a wide range of consumers. The chipmaker designed the standard Workstation Edition and the more power-efficient Max-Q Workstation Edition for the professional segment. In contrast, the data center-oriented Server Edition targets large-scale enterprises.Nvidia launched the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell and RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q in March 2025. With a launch price of $8,565, it was always a premium product. Nonetheless, many consumers sought out the RTX Pro 6000 for its impressive performance in professional workloads.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: With Nvidia now asking $13,250 for the RTX Pro 6000, it has gone up by a whopping 55%. Nvidia lists the PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell at $11,359.99, which is 14% less than the MSRP. However, it’s important to note that these prices are the official listings for the RTX Pro 6000 on Nvidia’s marketplace; while a good reference, they do not always reflect the lowest available prices.Nvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell PricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyGraphics CardNvidiaNeweggB&H Photo VideoNvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell ServerN/A$14,999.00N/ANvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell$13,250.00$12,099.99$13,349.00Nvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q$13,250.00$14,999.00$13,999.00PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell$11,359.99$13,645.00$14,499.00PNY RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-QN/AN/A$13,999.00For instance, Newegg currently sells the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell for $12,099.99, offering a 9% savings compared to Nvidia’s official marketplace price. When it comes to the other variants of the RTX Pro 6000, Nvidia’s marketplace offers lower prices than those found on Newegg or B&H Photo Video. Sometimes retailers are implementing significant markups, so it's more affordable to shop at Nvidia for certain models. While Nvidia doesn’t appear to offer the server variant directly to the general public, Newegg has it listed for a steep $14,999. history.author: Zhiye Liu
history.titleZh: 英伟达将RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU定价提升至13250美元——一年内较建议零售价上涨55%
history.descriptionZh:
全球内存短缺与人工智能热潮持续推高显卡价格，即便是市面上最好的游戏或工作站级显卡也难以幸免。Nvidia 悄然将其 Blackwell 工作站系列旗舰产品 RTX Pro 6000 的价格提升至令人咋舌的 13,250 美元，较一年前的原始发布价上涨了 55%。

Nvidia 提供了三款不同的 RTX Pro 6000 变体，以满足广泛的用户需求。标准版工作站型号（Workstation Edition）和更节能的 Max-Q 工作站型号（Max-Q Workstation Edition）针对专业领域设计，而面向数据中心的服务器版本（Server Edition）则瞄准大型企业客户。

Nvidia 于 2025 年 3 月发布了 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 和 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Max-Q，当时首发价为 8,565 美元，这本身已属于高端产品。然而，许多用户因其在专业工作负载中的强劲性能而仍然选择购买。如今 Nvidia 将价格上调至 13,250 美元，涨幅高达 55%。Nvidia 官方市场列表上，PNY 品牌的 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 标价为 11,359.99 美元，比官方建议零售价低 14%。但需注意，这些价格是 Nvidia 官方市场的参考价，并不总是代表最低售价。

价格对比显示，不同零售商和渠道差异显著。例如，Newegg 目前将 RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell 标价为 12,099.99 美元，比 Nvidia 官方市场价便宜 9%。而对于其他变体，Nvidia 官方市场的价格反而低于 Newegg 或 B&H Photo Video 等零售商，有时零售商会大幅加价。Nvidia 似乎不直接向公众出售服务器版本，但 Newegg 上的第三方卖家将其标价为 14,999 美元。部分列表来自 Newegg 上的第三方卖家，其他标注为 OEM 产品——这些通常面向系统集成商或企业客户，以批量形式销售，不附带精美零售包装。

短期内，无论是消费级游戏显卡还是专业工作站显卡，价格都不太可能改善。唯一确定的趋势是：价格将继续攀升，直到内存短缺问题得到解决。不同零售商甚至官方市场（如 Nvidia 官网）之间的价格差异巨大，最佳策略是在购买前多方比价。

history.title: The future of Hollywood isn’t feeding prompts into vanilla gen AI models
history.description:
Concept art from Dear Upstairs Neighbors that used to train custom builds of Google’s Veo and Imagen models. | Image: Google DeepMind	

For all the noise that's been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven't really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the sort of entertainment people would pay to see. Most AI firms' video models are still only capable of churning out short bursts of visually inconsistent footage. And some of Hollywood's biggest AI partnerships have suddenly evaporated in ways that make it seem like studios might not be able to rely on the new technology coming out of Silicon Valley. For the most part, short-form video slop appears to be the only thing that major production houses ar …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: For all the noise that’s been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven’t really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the sort of entertainment people would pay to see. Most AI firms’ video models are still only capable of churning out short bursts of visually inconsistent footage. And some of Hollywood’s biggest AI partnerships have suddenly evaporated in ways that make it seem like studios might not be able to rely on the new technology coming out of Silicon Valley. For the most part, short-form video slop appears to be the only thing that major production houses are capable of cooking up with gen AI. But that could change if studios take notes from some of the experimental projects that debuted at this year’s Tribeca Film Festival.Though it feels unlikely that gen AI will ever be able to whip up a compelling movie whole cloth, Tribeca featured a number of films that demonstrated how human artists can leverage the technology in compelling ways.While none of the AI-powered movies that screened at Tribeca were as terrible as the video slop companies like OpenAI and xAI have polluted the internet with, some of the projects were prime examples of why generative content tends to feel so lifeless compared to human-crafted art. Roar — an animated short produced by Illuminai Studios — felt more like a disorienting montage of AI-generated clips rather than a cohesive piece of cinema. And Asteria Film Co.’s ChikaBOOM! lacked the visual and sonic polish that’s necessary for fast-paced fantasy about a magician in training to really pull you in.Roar and ChikaBOOM!’s overall roughness seemed to be a reflection of the inherent technological limitations baked into their respective AI-forward production workflows. But other films, like Google DeepMind’s Dear Upstairs Neighbors and OpenAI’s Mauvais Soleil, showcased how it’s possible for filmmakers to avoid those challenges when gen AI is deployed with a bit more ingenuity.Written and directed by Pixar veteran Connie Qin He in collaboration with researchers from Google DeepMind, Dear Upstairs Neighbors tells the story of an exhausted young woman who’s trying to go to bed. All Ada (Márcia Mayer, who also produced the short) wants is to get a couple hours of peaceful rest before she has to wake up and get back to work. But every time she begins dozing off, the cacophony of noise coming from her upstairs neighbors’ apartment jolts her awake and leaves her wondering what they could possibly be doing in the middle of the night.To give Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ world a distinct style, He enlisted Pixar production designer Yingzong Xin, who painted concept art in Photoshop and on paper using acrylics. Those illustrations’ expressionistic aesthetic was key to bringing Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ fantastical story to life, but it also presented a unique challenge to DeepMind’s researchers. With most AI video generation models, the illustrations’ painterly style would be difficult to turn into visually consistent footage. But DeepMind’s engineers developed custom versions of Veo and Imagen that were specifically designed to give Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ artists the ability to fine-tune their outputs.You can see how filmmakers have to work around some of gen AI’s more typical limitations.Because the customized models were trained on Xin’s concept art, they could consistently generate shots that adhered to He’s vision for the project. The text-to-video models were great at reproducing certain stylistic details, like the way sound is visualized when objects interact with one another. But to really build Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ scenes in a way that would tell a cohesive story, the short’s creative team had to do things a bit more traditionally. By creating rough animations with Autodesk Maya (the industry standard for 3D rigging and VFX), Dear Upstairs Neighbors’ production team could ensure that scenes would unfold exactly how they wanted them to. And by feeding those roughs into Veo, the artists could create scenes that were more visually polished and ready to be further enhanced with additional stylized assets generated with Veo and Imagen.More than any other film at Tribeca, Dear Upstairs Neighbors felt like a case study in how generative AI can be used as a bespoke tool that actually assists artists as they develop their ideas. The film’s entire workflow relied on human-made art and people making the kinds of nuanced creative decisions that text-to-video generators aren’t capable of on their own. It’s important to bear in mind that Dear Upstairs Neighbors would not be nearly as visually impressive if it had been produced with vanilla versions of Google’s various models. The models worked well for this particular short, but that’s to be expected for a project that’s also very much a commercial for Google’s technology.Image: Google DeepMindDear Upstairs Neighbors was a much more enjoyable watch than the films OpenAI brought to this year’s festival. Alice Gu’s semi-autobiographical drama Smoked used Sora to re-create the Palisades Fire, and Youssef Michraf’s Mauvais Soleil features a number of photorealistic scenes generated with OpenAI’s creative tools. Watching both films, you could see how their respective filmmakers had to work around some of gen AI’s more typical limitations. Wide shots in Smoked’s fiery scenes looked a bit cartoony, but the effect worked somewhat better in close-ups — which were filmed using a Volume-like setup — of a woman and her son trying to escape the blaze in their car. Most of Mauvais Soleil’s shots last for just a few seconds and the only speaking character is an unseen narrator, but the film’s story about how a man’s life is being warped by artificial intelligence makes those details feel like intentional artistic choices.OpenAI’s presence at Tribeca was somewhat surprising given the company’s recent decision to shut Sora down entirely. Sora’s sudden shuttering is what led to OpenAI’s feature-length film Critterz not being able to make its debut at this year’s Cannes Film Festival. It seems like OpenAI might be pivoting away from video-focused applications of its tech, but there are still other players in the gen AI space building tools that filmmakers can use to realize their projects.These workflows really only function well when they’re guided by human artists.With just $2,000 spent entirely on computing costs, writer / director Ash Koosha was able to singlehandedly produce Dreams of Violets, a docudrama focused on the nationwide protests that have rocked Iran throughout the past year. Using Kling AI, Claude, Gemini, and Nano Banana, Koosha tells a fictionalized story about a group of people who find themselves trapped in an alley as police stalk the streets brutalizing civilians. The project took Koosha just a few weeks to finish by himself, and while it’s supported by a powerful narrative, it doesn’t break any ground visually.Watching all of these films, I got the distinct sense that there is no future where studios are cranking out commercially viable projects by feeding prompts to gen AI models. That kind of content probably isn’t going to go away, but it’s not the kind of stuff Hollywood’s heavyweights would want to put their names on. What seems much more likely is bigger AI firms like Google partnering with studios to build bespoke models that are tailored to very specific workflows. And those workflows really only function well when they’re guided by human artists with very clear creative visions.
history.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
history.titleZh: 好莱坞的未来不是向普通生成式AI模型输入提示词
history.descriptionZh:
生成式AI一直被宣传为将彻底改变电影行业，但迄今为止，几乎没有用这项技术制作出让人愿意付费观看的娱乐作品。大多数AI公司的视频模型仍只能生成时长极短、视觉风格不连贯的片段。好莱坞一些最大的AI合作项目突然消失，也让人怀疑制片厂是否还能依赖硅谷的新技术。总体来看，大型制片公司用生成式AI能搞出来的似乎只有短视频“垃圾”。不过，如果制片厂能从今年翠贝卡电影节上展出的实验性项目中吸取经验，这一局面或许会改变。

虽然生成式AI似乎不太可能凭空创作出引人入胜的完整电影，但翠贝卡电影节上的多部影片展示了人类艺术家如何以巧妙的方式利用这项技术。这些AI驱动影片没有像OpenAI和xAI等公司污染互联网的视频“垃圾”那样糟糕，但有些项目也恰恰揭示了为什么生成式内容往往比人类创作的艺术显得更缺乏生命力。例如，Illuminai Studios制作的动画短片《Roar》更像一段令人困惑的AI生成片段蒙太奇，而非连贯的电影作品；Asteria Film Co.的《ChikaBOOM!》则缺乏视觉和声音上的打磨，难以吸引观众沉浸在一个关于见习魔法师的快节奏奇幻故事中。

《Roar》和《ChikaBOOM!》的粗糙感似乎反映了各自AI优先制作流程中固有的技术局限。而其他影片，如Google DeepMind的《亲爱的楼上邻居》（Dear Upstairs Neighbors）和OpenAI的《坏太阳》（Mauvais Soleil），则展示了当生成式AI被更巧妙地部署时，电影制作人能够规避这些挑战。

《亲爱的楼上邻居》由皮克斯资深人士秦何（Connie Qin He）编剧并执导，与Google DeepMind的研究人员合作完成。影片讲述了一位疲惫的年轻女性试图入睡的故事。主角艾达（由制片人Márcia Mayer配音）只想在起床继续工作前安静休息几个小时，但楼上邻居公寓传来的嘈杂噪音每次都把她惊醒，让她怀疑他们半夜到底在干什么。为了给影片的世界赋予独特风格，秦何邀请了皮克斯制作设计师辛英宗（Yingzong Xin）用Photoshop和丙烯画纸创作概念艺术。这些插画的表現主义美学是赋予影片奇幻故事生命力的关键，但也给DeepMind的研究人员带来了独特挑战。大多数AI视频生成模型难以将这种绘画风格转化为视觉一致的镜头。然而，DeepMind的工程师开发了定制版本的Veo和Imagen，专门让影片的艺术家能够精细调整输出结果。

由于定制模型是在辛的概念艺术上训练的，它们能够一致地生成符合秦何项目愿景的镜头。这些文本到视频模型擅长再现某些风格细节，比如物体相互作用时声音的可视化方式。但为了真正构建出能讲述连贯故事的场景，短片创作团队不得不采用更传统的方法。通过使用Autodesk Maya（3D蒙皮和视觉特效的行业标准）制作粗略动画，团队确保场景按预期展开。然后将这些粗略动画输入Veo，艺术家就能创建视觉上更精致的场景，并进一步用Veo和Imagen生成的额外风格化资产进行增强。

在翠贝卡电影节的众多影片中，《亲爱的楼上邻居》最像是一个案例研究，展示了生成式AI如何作为定制工具真正协助艺术家发展创意。整部影片的工作流程都依赖于人类创作的艺术以及人类做出的细微创意决策——这是文本到视频生成器无法独立完成的。值得注意的是，如果使用谷歌模型的原生版本，《亲爱的楼上邻居》在视觉上绝不会如此令人印象深刻。这些模型对这个特定短片很有效，但这也在意料之中，毕竟该项目本身也是谷歌技术的宣传片。

相比之下，OpenAI带来的影片观赏体验稍逊。艾丽斯·古（Alice Gu）的半自传体剧情片《Smoked》使用Sora再现了帕利塞兹大火；尤瑟夫·米沙拉夫（Youssef Michraf）的《坏太阳》则包含大量用OpenAI创意工具生成的逼真场景。观看这两部影片时，可以看到电影制作人如何规避生成式AI的一些典型局限。《Smoked》中火灾场景的广角镜头看起来有点卡通化，但在一个女人和儿子试图驾车逃离火灾的特写镜头中——这些镜头是用类似Volume的装置拍摄的——效果反而稍好。《坏太阳》的大多数镜头只持续几秒，唯一的对白角色是一位看不见的旁白，但影片关于一个男人的生活被人工智能扭曲的故事，让这些细节显得像是刻意的艺术选择。

OpenAI出现在翠贝卡电影节有些令人惊讶，因为该公司最近决定彻底关闭Sora。Sora的突然关闭正是导致OpenAI的长片《Critterz》无法在今年戛纳电影节首映的原因。OpenAI似乎正在远离其技术的视频应用，但生成式AI领域还有其他玩家在开发电影制作人可用于实现项目的工具。

作家兼导演阿什·库沙（Ash Koosha）仅用了2000美元计算成本，就独立制作了纪录片式剧情片《紫罗兰之梦》（Dreams of Violets），聚焦过去一年席卷伊朗的全国性抗议。他使用Kling AI、Claude、Gemini和Nano Banana，讲述了一个虚构故事：一群人发现自己被困在一条小巷中，而警察在街上横行，残暴对待平民。该项目仅用了几周时间就由他独自完成，虽然叙事有力，但在视觉上并未突破。

观看所有这些影片后，我得到一个清晰的感觉：制片厂通过向生成式AI模型输入提示语来产出商业上可行的项目的未来并不存在。那种内容可能不会消失，但好莱坞巨头不会愿意在这种东西上署名。更可能的是，像谷歌这样的大型AI公司与制片厂合作，构建针对特定工作流程的定制模型。而这些工作流程只有在非常清晰创意愿景的人类艺术家引导下，才能真正良好运作。

history.title: Dev releases ‘unblockable’ ASCII video stream software, stoking fears of unstoppable ads — delivers 360p video at 30 FPS and acts as a ‘bridge for AI’
history.description: A new and unique video streaming solution is pitched as a 'high-performance, real-time ASCII video rendering engine' that can be used to broadcast 'an unblockable video stream.'
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new and unique ASCII video streaming solution has been released under the MIT license. ASCILINE Engine by YusufB5 is pitched as a “high-performance, real-time ASCII video rendering engine” that can be used to broadcast “an unblockable video stream.” Examples of its capabilities are provided in the linked GitHub repository and social media posts by the dev, like the one embedded below. It's also stoked a bit of controversy due to fears of it potentially enabling unblockable ads.I built an unblockable video stream. It renders 360p at 30 FPS using pure text instead of tags. from r/SideProjectFrom our perusal of the examples, ASCILINE does indeed look like it is capable of better fidelity than prior video to ASCII streamers, some of which have a surprisingly long history dating back to the 90s. The software does a pretty decent job of making color text-based videos from a source. The dev notes that this technique uses Mode 3, using a palette of 32K colors, and can output at 30 FPS. Though classic mono ASCII is also a render option.Most impressive is the so-called real-time pixel streaming. The GitHub explains that this technique uses Mode 5 and “replaces characters with colored blocks, approaching 360p video quality.” Indeed, in the small embedded video on the page, it looks indistinguishable from the source MP4. We think the innate blockiness would become apparent quickly if it were rendered in a larger window, though.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: YusufB5 on GitHub)In a mission statement of sorts, YusufB5 says that ASCILINE’s “core objective is to transform the web into a highly dynamic and interactive typographic canvas. By mapping pixels to text-based representations, we unlock new possibilities for web media delivery.” On social media, the dev simplifies this highfalutin vision by heralding ASCILINE Engine as a tool that can build “an unblockable video stream. It renders 360p at 30 FPS using pure text.”The ‘unblockable’ claim gets plenty of pushback across various social media channels. For example, an adblocker put in element zapper mode can quickly remove the HTML5 Canvas that the ASCII video renders in. That’s just one option. You may like Developer creates a basic first person shooter game using Gaussian splats, and you can play it for free in your browser Nvidia's own DLSS 5 announcement video gets taken down by YouTube in Italy due to a copyright strike UK spy agency releases malware-blocking gadget for HDMI and DisplayPort cables However, most commenters are fearful of those wanting to serve more ads to the public using this ‘unblockable’ tech. YusufB5 points to their “strict anti-ad clause to the MIT License to ensure this isn't abused to force unskippable ads on people.” That might work with registered companies, but malicious users won’t care about such a rule.Beyond the unblockability and ad misuse controversies, ASCILINE has some more interesting frills and features to set it apart from the old guard of ASCII video. The ability to apply real-time CSS filters to the video stream sounds like it may be appealing in some instances. Also, the ASCII video generated is thought to “act as a perfect bridge for AI.” Thus, you can use lightweight LLMs to process semantic video summaries. The engine’s “ultra-low bandwidth & IoT compatibility” may also be attractive to some, with its ability to stream at only a few kilobytes per frame. Sending only the characters that change (delta frames) and applying GZIP compression both help in this regard.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: 开发者发布“不可屏蔽”的ASCII视频流软件，引发对无法阻挡的广告的担忧——以30 FPS传输360p视频，并充当“人工智能的桥梁”
history.descriptionZh:
基于MIT许可协议发布的新型ASCII视频流解决方案——ASCILINE Engine，由开发者YusufB5推出，被宣传为一款“高性能、实时ASCII视频渲染引擎”，能够传输“不可阻挡的视频流”。该项目已在GitHub上开源，并引发了关于其可能被滥用于推送不可阻挡广告的争议。

### 背景与上下文
传统的ASCII视频流技术可追溯至20世纪90年代，但受限于分辨率和色彩表现。ASCILINE Engine试图突破这些限制，通过纯文本字符映射实现接近标准视频的质量。开发者声称其核心目标是“将网页转变为高度动态、交互式的排版画布”，从而为网页媒体传输解锁新可能。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
引擎支持两种渲染模式：
- **Mode 3（经典ASCII模式）**：使用32K色彩调色板（15位色），以30帧/秒输出彩色字符视频。该模式保留了传统ASCII艺术风格，但显著提升了色彩精度。
- **Mode 5（实时像素流模式）**：将字符替换为彩色方块，以“缺失分辨率换取流畅度”，实现接近360p的视频质量。在该模式下，字符网格中的每个单元格被填充为纯色块，从而模拟像素视觉。演示视频显示，在小窗口渲染中，其效果与原始MP4几乎无法区分，但放大后锯齿感可能显露。

创新点包括：
- **增量帧传输**：仅发送发生变化的字符（delta frames），结合GZIP压缩，将每帧数据传输量降至数KB级别，实现超低带宽消耗。
- **物联网兼容性**：由于数据量极小，可在低功耗设备或窄带网络上流畅运行。
- **实时CSS滤镜**：支持对视频流应用CSS过滤器，提供动态视觉效果调整能力。
- **AI友好接口**：生成的结构化文本视频可作为轻量级大语言模型（LLM）处理语义摘要的“完美桥梁”，允许基于文本形式的视频内容分析。

### 性能参数与对比数据
- 分辨率：Mode 5模式下可达360p（约480×360字符网格，每个字符视为像素块）。
- 帧率：稳定30 FPS，与标准视频流一致。
- 带宽：每帧仅需数KB（压缩后），远低于传统视频流（通常需数百KB至数MB）。
- 色彩：Mode 3支持32000色；Mode 5使用有限色块（像素块颜色取自源视频平均色）。

与历史ASCII视频方案相比，ASCILINE在色彩保真度和实时性上有显著提升，尤其Mode 5的像素块渲染技术实现了“视觉上难以区分”的效果，这在此前是难以做到的。

### 技术影响与应用场景
1. **“不可阻挡”的争议**：开发者宣称该引擎可构建“不可阻挡的视频流”，因为内容以纯文本形式传输，传统广告拦截器难以识别。然而，技术社区指出，广告拦截器可通过“元素消隐”模式（如删除HTML5 Canvas元素）轻松阻止渲染。尽管如此，核心担忧在于该技术可能被滥用于强制推送无法跳过的广告。开发者已在MIT许可中加入了“严格反广告条款”，但该条款对恶意用户缺乏约束力。
2. **潜在合法应用**：
   - **低带宽直播**：适用于网络条件受限的场景（如农村、车载、IoT设备），仅需基础文本通道即可传输视频。
   - **AI视频摘要**：将视频转换为文本流后，可由轻量级LLM实时生成语义摘要，实现无障碍访问或快速浏览。
   - **创意艺术**：生成具有ASCII美学的动态视频，可用于复古界面、数字艺术或特殊视觉效果。
   - **教育工具**：无需复杂编解码器即可展示视频内容，适合浏览器兼容性要求高的环境。

### 社区反馈与未来展望
尽管技术演示令人印象深刻，但质疑声集中于实际可用性。例如，360p分辨率在放大后明显不足，且实时渲染依赖HTML5 Canvas，导致其“不可阻挡”特性易被绕开。然而，ASCILINE Engine展示了将视频降维至文本层的新思路，可能启发未来更多文本化媒体处理方案。目前，项目已获得Reddit、Tom's Hardware等科技媒体关注，成为ASCII视频演进史上的一个标志性节点。

history.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Leads on First Agentic AI Infrastructure Benchmark
history.description: AgentPerf from Artificial Analysis, the industry’s first agentic AI benchmark, gives developers, enterprises and infrastructure providers a clear way to compare systems for agentic AI. In the first round of published results, the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72 platform delivers leading performance across the agentic AI workloads tested, running 20x more agents per megawatt than NVIDIA […]
history.content: AgentPerf from Artificial Analysis, the industry’s first agentic AI benchmark, gives developers, enterprises and infrastructure providers a clear way to compare systems for agentic AI. In the first round of published results, the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72 platform delivers leading performance across the agentic AI workloads tested, running 20x more agents per megawatt than NVIDIA Hopper. Agentic AI is a fundamentally different workload than conversational AI. A single chat completion is a sprint: one large language model (LLM) call, one response. An agent functions more like a relay: It breaks a goal into many steps and keeps going until the task is done. Agents chain together multiple LLM calls and tool calls to gather context, observe, reason and act. That results in dozens to hundreds of LLM calls chained together, each passing growing context to the next, with tool calls like code compile and execution, database search and web browsing at every handoff. The complexity isn’t additive; it’s multiplicative. The distinction matters enormously for performance measurement. Existing AI inference benchmarks measure one LLM call: how fast an LLM responds to a single request and how many simultaneous requests a system can handle. They weren’t designed for agentic workloads, where chained LLM calls, tool call delays and growing context stress accelerated computing systems in fundamentally different ways than a single LLM call ever could. For companies building and deploying agents at scale, it’s important to understand how responsive agents are, how many can be deployed simultaneously and how much useful work AI infrastructure can deliver for every dollar and watt invested. NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 Runs 20x More Agents per Megawatt In this first round, AgentPerf measures agentic performance with DeepSeek V4 Pro, a large mixture-of-experts (MoE) model that represents the class of frontier models powering today’s most capable agents. On this workload, NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 delivers the highest performance in the benchmark, running up to 20x more agents per megawatt than the NVIDIA HGX H200 system. NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 supports far more concurrent agents per megawatt than NVIDIA H200 at both service-level objectives of 20 and 60 tokens per second per agent. The performance advantage comes from extreme codesign across the full stack. GB300 NVL72 connects 72 GPUs into a single rack-scale system, enabling large MoE models like DeepSeek V4 Pro to distribute model execution efficiently at scale. CUDA kernels accelerate this further by overlapping communication and compute, so the cost of coordinating across experts is absorbed rather than added to latency. NVIDIA TensorRT LLM sustains efficiency as concurrent agent sessions scale. For example, it separates the processing of inputs from the generation of outputs so each can be optimized independently. These results are grounded in a benchmark methodology built from the ground up to reflect how agentic AI actually works in production. Artificial Analysis AgentPerf: Built on Real-World Agentic Workloads AgentPerf is built based on real coding agent trajectories: an agent receives a task, reads files, writes and edits code, executes commands and iterates based on the results — all drawn from real public code repositories across 12+ programming languages. The long sequence lengths, tool call patterns and delays are all representative of real-world coding workflows. AgentPerf then measures how many of these agentic tasks a platform can support simultaneously while meeting defined performance thresholds for responsiveness and output token rate. Tool calls are not executed but simulated using representative CPU processing time, so differences in results reflect accelerated computing performance only. The results translate directly into infrastructure decisions: how many concurrent agentic tasks can be run per accelerator and per megawatt of power. For enterprises deploying AI agents at scale, those numbers determine how much productive work a given infrastructure investment can actually deliver. NVIDIA Ecosystem Partners Harness Blackwell’s Leading Performance Leading inference providers including Baseten, DeepInfra and Together AI are already serving agentic workloads on frontier models such as DeepSeek V4 Pro on NVIDIA Blackwell and powering production agentic applications today. Together AI powers real-time inference for Cursor, an AI-powered agentic coding platform, on NVIDIA Blackwell. Cursor’s agents debug issues, generate features and execute refactors while developers continue working. DeepInfra powers Pam.ai, an AI workforce platform for car dealerships, which deploys agents to book service appointments, handle calls and run outbound sales campaigns, entirely on NVIDIA Blackwell. As NVIDIA and the open source ecosystem continue to optimize inference software, performance and efficiency on agentic workloads will only improve. The NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture is now in full production, bringing the next generation of infrastructure capacity to meet the growing demands of agentic AI at scale. Dive deeper into AgentPerf’s methodology and NVIDIA’s full-stack optimizations for agentic AI in this technical blog.
history.author: Shruti Koparkar
history.titleZh: NVIDIA Blackwell 在首个代理型 AI 基础设施基准测试中领先
history.descriptionZh:
业界首个智能体AI（Agentic AI）基准测试AgentPerf由Artificial Analysis推出，为开发者、企业和基础设施提供商提供了一种清晰对比智能体AI系统性能的方式。在首轮公布的结果中，NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra NVL72平台在测试的智能体AI工作负载上取得了领先性能，每兆瓦功耗可运行20倍于NVIDIA Hopper的智能体数量。

智能体AI与对话式AI是截然不同的工作负载。单次对话补全如同短跑：一次大语言模型（LLM）调用，一次响应。而智能体更像是接力赛：它将一个目标分解为多个步骤，不断执行直到任务完成。智能体会串联多次LLM调用和工具调用，以收集上下文、观察、推理并采取行动。这导致数十到数百次LLM调用链条式连接，每次调用都在传递不断增长的上下文，并在每次交接时执行代码编译、执行、数据库搜索、网页浏览等工具调用。其复杂度不是相加的，而是相乘的。

这种差异对性能测量至关重要。现有的AI推理基准只测量单次LLM调用：衡量LLM对单一请求的响应速度以及系统能同时处理多少并发请求。它们并非为智能体工作负载设计，因为链式LLM调用、工具调用延迟和不断增长的上下文对加速计算系统的压力方式与单次LLM调用有本质不同。对于大规模构建和部署智能体的企业来说，了解智能体的响应速度、能同时部署多少个、以及每投入一美元和每瓦特功耗能产出的有用工作量，至关重要。

NVIDIA GB300 NVL72每兆瓦运行20倍智能体数
在本轮测试中，AgentPerf使用DeepSeek V4 Pro（一款大型混合专家模型，代表当前驱动最强大智能体的前沿模型类别）来衡量智能体性能。在该工作负载下，NVIDIA GB300 NVL72在基准测试中实现了最高性能，每兆瓦功耗运行的智能体数量达到NVIDIA HGX H200系统的20倍。在每智能体每秒20个token和60个token两个服务水平目标下，GB300 NVL72每兆瓦支持的并发智能体数均远超H200。

这一性能优势源于全栈极致协同设计。GB300 NVL72将72块GPU连接成一个机架级系统，使DeepSeek V4 Pro这类大型MoE模型能够高效地大规模分布式执行。CUDA内核进一步通过重叠通信和计算来加速，使得跨专家协调的成本被吸收而非叠加到延迟中。NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM在并发智能体会话扩展时保持效率，例如它将输入处理与输出生成分离，使两者可独立优化。

这些结果根植于一个从零开始构建、反映智能体AI在生产中实际运作方式的基准方法。

AgentPerf：基于真实世界智能体工作负载构建
AgentPerf基于真实编码智能体的轨迹构建：智能体接收任务、读取文件、编写和编辑代码、执行命令并根据结果迭代——所有这些都来自12种以上编程语言的真实公共代码仓库。长序列长度、工具调用模式和延迟均代表真实编码工作流。然后AgentPerf衡量一个平台能在满足响应性和输出token率定义阈值的同时，并行支持多少此类智能体任务。工具调用不实际执行，而是用代表性的CPU处理时间模拟，因此结果差异仅反映加速计算性能。

结果直接转化为基础设施决策：每个加速器和每兆瓦功耗可运行多少并发智能体任务。对于大规模部署AI智能体的企业，这些数字决定了给定基础设施投资能实际产生多少有效工作量。

NVIDIA生态伙伴利用Blackwell领先性能
领先的推理提供商如Baseten、DeepInfra和Together AI已经在NVIDIA Blackwell上使用DeepSeek V4 Pro等前沿模型为智能体工作负载提供服务，并驱动生产级智能体应用。Together AI为AI编码平台Cursor在Blackwell上提供实时推理，Cursor的智能体能在开发者继续工作的同时调试问题、生成功能和执行重构。DeepInfra为汽车经销商的AI劳动力平台Pam.ai提供支持，该平台在Blackwell上部署智能体来预约服务、处理电话和进行外呼营销。

随着NVIDIA和开源生态系统继续优化推理软件，智能体工作负载的性能和效率将持续提升。NVIDIA Vera Rubin架构现已全面投产，为满足大规模智能体AI日益增长的需求带来了下一代基础设施能力。更多关于AgentPerf方法及NVIDIA全栈优化的细节可参阅技术博客。
